title: लक्ष्मी नारायण स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री लक्ष्मी नारायण स्तोत्रम् ॥

लक्ष्मीनारायणं वन्दे सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम्।
श्रिया सहासीनं देवं वैकुण्ठे परमं पदम्॥ १॥

क्षीरसागरमध्यस्थं लक्ष्मीनारायणं प्रभुम्।
शेषशायिनमानन्दं वन्दे श्रीपतिमव्ययम्॥ २॥

चतुर्भुजं गदापाणिं शंखचक्रधरं हरिम्।
लक्ष्म्या सव्यांकसंस्थायां वन्दे नारायणं सदा॥ ३॥

लक्ष्मी कराभ्यामभयं वरदां
पद्मद्वयं धारयतीं सुशोभाम्।
नारायणाङ्कस्थितमातरं तां
वन्दे सदा विश्वजनीमनन्ताम्॥ ४॥

यत्र लक्ष्मीश्च विष्णुश्च तत्र श्रीर्विजयो ध्रुवम्।
तत्र धर्मश्च कीर्तिश्च न्यायश्चैव सनातनः॥ ५॥

लक्ष्मीनारायणौ देवौ सर्वसंपत्प्रदायकौ।
गृहे यत्र पूजितौ स्यातां तत्र सर्वं शुभं भवेत्॥ ६॥

श्रियं च पतिं श्रियाः सर्वमंगलकारिणम्।
ध्यायेद् नित्यं प्रभाते च दम्पती परमेश्वरौ॥ ७॥

लक्ष्मीनारायणप्रीत्यै पठेद्भक्त्या तु यो नरः।
धनधान्यसमृद्धिश्च गृहे तस्य सदा वसेत्॥ ८॥

दारिद्र्यदुःखशमनं गृहक्लेशनिवारणम्।
लक्ष्मीनारायणस्तोत्रं सर्वसौभाग्यवर्धनम्॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री लक्ष्मी नारायण स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Lakshmi Narayana Stotram ||

Lakshmi-Narayanam vande sarva-loka-maheshvaram |
Shriya sahasinam devam Vaikunthe paramam padam || 1 ||

Kshira-sagara-madhyastham Lakshmi-Narayanam prabhum |
Shesha-shayinam-anandam vande Shripatim-avyayam || 2 ||

Chatur-bhujam gada-panim shankha-chakra-dharam Harim |
Lakshmya savyanka-samsthayam vande Narayanam sada || 3 ||

Lakshmi karabhyam-abhayam varadam
Padma-dvayam dharayatim su-shobham |
Narayananka-sthita-mataram tam
Vande sada vishva-janim-anantam || 4 ||

Yatra Lakshmishcha Vishnushcha tatra shrir-vijayo dhruvam |
Tatra dharmashcha kirtishcha nyayashchaiva sanatanh || 5 ||

Lakshmi-Narayanau devau sarva-sampat-pradayakau |
Grihe yatra pujitau syatam tatra sarvam shubham bhavet || 6 ||

Shriyam cha patim shriyah sarva-mangala-karinam |
Dhyayed nityam prabhate cha dampati Parameshvarau || 7 ||

Lakshmi-Narayana-prityai pathed-bhaktya tu yo narah |
Dhana-dhanya-samriddhishcha grihe tasya sada vaset || 8 ||

Daridrya-duhkha-shamanam griha-klesha-nivaranam |
Lakshmi-Narayana-stotram sarva-saubhagya-vardhanam || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Lakshmi Narayana Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
सर्वलोक के महेश्वर लक्ष्मी-नारायण को वन्दन। श्री (लक्ष्मी) के साथ विराजमान, वैकुण्ठ में परम पद पर स्थित देव को प्रणाम॥ १॥

क्षीर सागर के मध्य में स्थित, शेष पर शयन करने वाले, आनन्दस्वरूप, अव्यय श्रीपति लक्ष्मीनारायण प्रभु को वन्दन॥ २॥

चतुर्भुज, गदापाणि, शंख-चक्र धारी हरि, लक्ष्मी जिनके बायें अंक में विराजमान हैं, उन नारायण को सदा वन्दन॥ ३॥

दोनों हाथों से अभय और वरद मुद्रा देती हुई, दो कमल धारण करती हुई, सुशोभा लक्ष्मी, नारायण के अंक में स्थित, विश्वजननी, अनन्त माता को सदा वन्दन॥ ४॥

जहाँ लक्ष्मी और विष्णु हैं, वहाँ श्री और विजय निश्चित हैं। वहाँ धर्म, कीर्ति और सनातन न्याय भी होता है॥ ५॥

लक्ष्मी-नारायण दोनों देव सर्व सम्पत्ति प्रदायक हैं। जिस घर में इनकी पूजा होती है, वहाँ सब शुभ होता है॥ ६॥

श्री (लक्ष्मी) और उनके पति, सर्वमंगलकारी को प्रातःकाल नित्य ध्यान करें - वे दम्पती परमेश्वर हैं॥ ७॥

जो मनुष्य लक्ष्मीनारायण की प्रीति के लिए भक्ति से इसका पाठ करता है, उसके घर में धन-धान्य समृद्धि सदा निवास करती है॥ ८॥

दरिद्रता और दुःख का शमन करने वाला, घर के क्लेश दूर करने वाला, सर्व सौभाग्यवर्धक यह लक्ष्मीनारायण स्तोत्र है॥ ९॥
benefits: Joint prayer to Lakshmi-Narayana for complete blessings
Invokes the blessing of wealth, prosperity, and abundance
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and household troubles
Traditionally associated with harmony and auspiciousness to married life
Invokes both material and spiritual fulfillment
Ideal for daily morning worship of the divine couple
description: The Lakshmi Narayana Stotram, known in Sanskrit as लक्ष्मी नारायण स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Vishnu. This revered text originates from Vaishnavite Traditional Literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant both material and spiritual fulfillment as well as bring harmony and auspiciousness to married life. Additionally, it is known to joint prayer to Lakshmi-Narayana for complete blessings. This prayer is traditionally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Ekadashi, Diwali, and Kartik Purnima to invoke divine grace. At 9 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Lakshmi Narayana Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Lakshmi Narayana Stotram - prayer to Lord Vishnu and Goddess Lakshmi together in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning.

seoFaqs.question: What is the scriptural source of Lakshmi Narayana Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The most widely recited Lakshmi Narayana Stotram is an eight-verse hymn (ashtakam) found in the Padma Purana, where it is described as having been first recited by Indra in praise of the divine couple. The Padma Purana is one of the major eighteen Mahapuranas, traditionally classified as a sattvika purana with strong Vaishnava content. The hymn appears as part of a longer narrative about Indra's worship of Lakshmi-Narayana following one of his crises, and has subsequently entered general devotional usage across India through liturgical compilations and stotra anthologies. Its association with Indra gives it particular weight in the wider Vaishnava devotional canon.

seoFaqs.question: Why are Lakshmi and Narayana praised together rather than separately?
seoFaqs.answer: In Vaishnava theology, Lakshmi is not merely Vishnu's consort but his shakti, the inseparable feminine pole of the supreme being. Together they are addressed as Lakshmi-Narayana, the indivisible cosmic couple from which all creation flows. The compound is meant to indicate that Vishnu without Lakshmi is incomplete and that Lakshmi without Vishnu is unanchored. The stotram captures this by describing them as forever young, the eternal parents of the world, and as inseparable as lightning within a cloud. Vaishnava devotional practice almost always invokes both names together, and even temples dedicated to one will hold a murti or image of the other.

seoFaqs.question: What spiritual fruit does the stotram promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti declares that the devotee who recites the eight verses will, after enjoying the pleasures of this world, certainly attain the joy of salvation (moksha). This dual promise is significant: the hymn does not ask the devotee to renounce worldly enjoyment in order to qualify for liberation, but presents the two as a continuous arc that Lakshmi-Narayana grant together. Lakshmi presides over the worldly half (artha and kama within dharma) and Narayana over the salvific half (moksha). The pairing of names corresponds exactly to the pairing of fruits, which is one reason this stotram is especially loved by householders rather than only by renunciates.

seoFaqs.question: When is Lakshmi Narayana Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: It is recited on Fridays (Lakshmi's day) and Saturdays (which in some traditions are dedicated to Vishnu in the form of Venkateswara), on Ekadashi, on Akshaya Tritiya which is considered the most auspicious wealth-day of the year, on Diwali night during Lakshmi puja, on Vaikuntha Ekadashi when the gates of Vaikuntha are said to open, and during the Margashirsha and Karthika months which are dear to both Lakshmi and Vishnu. Many household altars feature a single murti of Lakshmi-Narayana together, and the stotram is the standard daily prayer before this image. Newly married couples are often given the practice of reciting the stotram together as a marital sadhana.

seoFaqs.question: How does the stotram describe Vaikuntha?
seoFaqs.answer: Vaikuntha, the abode of Lakshmi-Narayana, is described in Vaishnava texts as a realm beyond the cycles of creation and destruction, made of pure consciousness and bliss, where Vishnu reclines on Ananta-Sesha in the milky ocean and Lakshmi attends to him in serene love. The Lakshmi Narayana Stotram alludes to this realm as the place to which the devotee aspires after worldly enjoyment, the joy of salvation referred to in the closing verse. Unlike svarga (heaven), which is temporary and exhausted by merit, Vaikuntha is described as the eternal abode from which there is no return to samsara. The stotram therefore functions as a small Vaikuntha-darshana, a brief mental pilgrimage to that abode.

seoFaqs.question: Are there longer Lakshmi Narayana texts beyond this short hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. The Lakshmi Narayana Ashtottara Shatanama Stotram lists 108 names of the divine couple and is widely recited in temples and homes. The Lakshmi Narayana Sahasranama, a thousand-name stotram, is preserved in some Vaishnava traditions for special occasions. There are also extensive Lakshmi-only and Vishnu-only hymns, such as the Vishnu Sahasranama and the Mahalakshmi Ashtakam, that complement the Lakshmi-Narayana hymns. Devotees often build a layered practice: the short ashtakam daily, the 108 names on Fridays, and the longer texts on major festivals. This layered approach is recommended in many Vaishnava manuals as the gradual deepening of stotra-bhakti over a lifetime.
